name: Assessment List for Trustworthy AI
owner: European Commission High-Level Expert Group on AI
description: Practical self-assessment tool for operationalizing the EU's Ethics Guidelines for Trustworthy AI. Organized around seven key requirements: human agency and oversight; technical robustness and safety; privacy and data governance; transparency; diversity, non-discrimination and fairness; societal and environmental wellbeing; accountability.
relationToAISecurity: Direct AI ethics self-assessment companion to the Ethics Guidelines.

license.notes: EU-published guidance. Reuse permitted under European Commission legal notice; attribution expected.

current_extraction.notes: Acquired the publisher's landing/overview page. May be just the public-facing page that links to detailed framework docs; deep-link acquisition is a follow-up if more depth is needed.

extraction_history.notes: Metadata-only stub. Source content acquisition + structured extraction is TODO.

notes: Metadata stub; acquire source and extract structured form as follow-up.

desired_end_state.notes: Captured content is the publisher's public landing/overview page describing the framework. The framework's underlying per-control/per-requirement text (where one exists) lives in publisher-hosted PDFs, GitHub repos, or member-only portals that would each need a separate acquisition path. Extracted markdown is the appropriate end-state for this stub; flip to 'structured' only if a discrete per-control source becomes available and is worth parsing.
